Leptorhoeo is a genus of flowering plants in the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the gentian family. The plants in this genus are native to the tropical regions of Asia and are known for their medicinal properties. Leptorhoeo plants contain bioactive constituents such as iridoid glycosides, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leptorhoeo plants include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
